verses.latin_vulgate: Anno autem quintodecimo imperii Tiberii Cæsaris, procurante Pontio Pilato Judæam, tetrarcha autem Galiææ Herode, Philippo autem fratre ejus tetrarcha Iturææ, et Trachonitidis regionis, et Lysania Abilinæ tetrarcha,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἔτει δὲ πεντεκαιδεκάτῳ τῆς ἡγεμονίας τιβερίου καίσαρος ἡγεμονεύοντος ποντίου πιλάτου τῆς ἰουδαίας καὶ τετραρχοῦντος τῆς γαλιλαίας ἡρῴδου φιλίππου δὲ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτοῦ τετραρχοῦντος, τῆς ἰτουραίας καὶ τραχωνίτιδος χώρας καὶ λυσανίου τῆς ἀβιληνῆς τετραρχοῦντος
verses.king_james: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judaea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituraea and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias the tetrarch of Abilene,

verses.latin_vulgate: sub principibus sacerdotum Anna et Caipha: factum est verbum Domini super Joannem, Zachariæ filium, in deserto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπ᾽ ἀρχιερέων ἅννα καὶ καϊάφα ἐγένετο ῥῆμα θεοῦ ἐπὶ ἰωάννην τὸν τοῦ ξαχαρίου υἱὸν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ
verses.king_james: Annas and Caiaphas being the high priests, the word of God came unto John the son of Zacharias in the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit in omnem regionem Jordanis, prædicans baptismum pœnitentiæ in remissionem peccatorum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦλθεν εἰς πᾶσαν τὴν περίχωρον τοῦ ἰορδάνου κηρύσσων βάπτισμα μετανοίας εἰς ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτιῶν
verses.king_james: And he came into all the country about Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut scriptum est in libro sermonum Isaiæ prophetæ: [Vox clamantis in deserto: Parate viam Domini; rectas facite semitas ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς γέγραπται ἐν βίβλῳ λόγων ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος, φωνὴ βοῶντος ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ἑτοιμάσατε τὴν ὁδὸν κυρίου εὐθείας ποιεῖτε τὰς τρίβους αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: As it is written in the book of the words of Esaias the prophet, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

verses.latin_vulgate: omnis vallis implebitur, et omnis mons, et collis humiliabitur: et erunt prava in directa, et aspera in vias planas:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσα φάραγξ πληρωθήσεται καὶ πᾶν ὄρος καὶ βουνὸς ταπεινωθήσεται καὶ ἔσται τὰ σκολιὰ εἰς εὐθείαν καὶ αἱ τραχεῖαι εἰς ὁδοὺς λείας
verses.king_james: Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low; and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways *shall be* made smooth;

verses.latin_vulgate: et videbit omnis caro salutare Dei.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὄψεται πᾶσα σὰρξ τὸ σωτήριον τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat ergo ad turbas quæ exibant ut baptizarentur ab ipso: Genimina viperarum, quis ostendit vobis fugere a ventura ira?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν οὖν τοῖς ἐκπορευομένοις ὄχλοις βαπτισθῆναι ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ γεννήματα ἐχιδνῶν τίς ὑπέδειξεν ὑμῖν φυγεῖν ἀπὸ τῆς μελλούσης ὀργῆς
verses.king_james: Then said he to the multitude that came forth to be baptized of him, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

verses.latin_vulgate: Facite ergo fructus dignos pœnitentiæ, et ne cœperitis dicere: Patrem habemus Abraham. Dico enim vobis quia potens est Deus de lapidibus istis suscitare filios Abrahæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποιήσατε οὖν καρποὺς ἀξίους τῆς μετανοίας καὶ μὴ ἄρξησθε λέγειν ἐν ἑαυτοῖς πατέρα ἔχομεν τὸν ἀβραάμ λέγω γὰρ ὑμῖν ὅτι δύναται ὁ θεὸς ἐκ τῶν λίθων τούτων ἐγεῖραι τέκνα τῷ ἀβραάμ
verses.king_james: Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and begin not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to *our* father: for I say unto you, That God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam enim securis ad radicem arborum posita est. Omnis ergo arbor non faciens fructum bonum, excidetur, et in ignem mittetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤδη δὲ καὶ ἡ ἀξίνη πρὸς τὴν ῥίζαν τῶν δένδρων κεῖται πᾶν οὖν δένδρον μὴ ποιοῦν καρπὸν καλὸν ἐκκόπτεται καὶ εἰς πῦρ βάλλεται
verses.king_james: And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogabant eum turbæ, dicentes: Quid ergo faciemus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν οἱ ὄχλοι λέγοντες τί οὖν ποιήσομεν
verses.king_james: And the people asked him, saying, What shall we do then?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem dicebat illis: Qui habet duas tunicas, det non habenti: et qui habet escas, similiter faciat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἔχων δύο χιτῶνας μεταδότω τῷ μὴ ἔχοντι καὶ ὁ ἔχων βρώματα ὁμοίως ποιείτω
verses.king_james: He answereth and saith unto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt autem et publicani ut baptizarentur, et dixerunt ad illum: Magister, quid faciemus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθον δὲ καὶ τελῶναι βαπτισθῆναι καὶ εἶπον πρὸς αὐτόν διδάσκαλε τί ποιήσομεν
verses.king_james: Then came also publicans to be baptized, and said unto him, Master, what shall we do?

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille dixit ad eos: Nihil amplius, quam quod constitutum est vobis, faciatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς μηδὲν πλέον παρὰ τὸ διατεταγμένον ὑμῖν πράσσετε
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Exact no more than that which is appointed you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interrogabant autem eum et milites, dicentes: Quid faciemus et nos? Et ait illis: Neminem concutiatis, neque calumniam faciatis: et contenti estote stipendiis vestris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπηρώτων δὲ αὐτὸν καὶ στρατευόμενοι λέγοντες καὶ ἡμεῖς τί ποιήσομεν καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς μηδένα διασείσητε μηδὲ συκοφαντήσητε καὶ ἀρκεῖσθε τοῖς ὀψωνίοις ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And the soldiers likewise demanded of him, saying, And what shall we do? And he said unto them, Do violence to no man, neither accuse *any* falsely; and be content with your wages.

verses.latin_vulgate: Existimante autem populo, et cogitantibus omnibus in cordibus suis de Joanne, ne forte ipse esset Christus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσδοκῶντος δὲ τοῦ λαοῦ καὶ διαλογιζομένων πάντων ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν περὶ τοῦ ἰωάννου μήποτε αὐτὸς εἴη ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: And as the people were in expectation, and all men mused in their hearts of John, whether he were the Christ, or not;

verses.latin_vulgate: respondit Joannes, dicens omnibus: Ego quidem aqua baptizo vos: veniet autem fortior me, cujus non sum dignus solvere corrigiam calceamentorum ejus: ipse vos baptizabit in Spiritu Sancto et igni:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίνατο ὁ ἰωάννης ἅπασιν λέγων, ἐγὼ μὲν ὕδατι βαπτίζω ὑμᾶς ἔρχεται δὲ ὁ ἰσχυρότερός μου οὗ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἱκανὸς λῦσαι τὸν ἱμάντα τῶν ὑποδημάτων αὐτοῦ αὐτὸς ὑμᾶς βαπτίσει ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ καὶ πυρί
verses.king_james: John answered, saying unto *them* all, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:

verses.latin_vulgate: cujus ventilabrum in manu ejus, et purgabit aream suam, et congregabit triticum in horreum suum, paleas autem comburet igni inextinguibili.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗ τὸ πτύον ἐν τῇ χειρὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ διακαθᾶριεῖ τὴν ἅλωνα αὐτοῦ καὶ συναξεῖ τὸν σῖτον εἰς τὴν ἀποθήκην αὐτοῦ τὸ δὲ ἄχυρον κατακαύσει πυρὶ ἀσβέστῳ
verses.king_james: Whose fan *is* in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner; but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multa quidem et alia exhortans evangelizabat populo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλὰ μὲν οὖν καὶ ἕτερα παρακαλῶν εὐηγγελίζετο τὸν λαόν
verses.king_james: And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Herodes autem tetrarcha cum corriperetur ab illo de Herodiade uxore fratris sui, et de omnibus malis quæ fecit Herodes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἡρῴδης ὁ τετράρχης, ἐλεγχόμενος ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ περὶ ἡρῳδιάδος τῆς γυναικὸς φιλίππου τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτοῦ καὶ περὶ πάντων ὧν ἐποίησεν πονηρῶν ὁ ἡρῴδης
verses.king_james: But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias his brother Philip's wife, and for all the evils which Herod had done,

verses.latin_vulgate: adjecit et hoc super omnia, et inclusit Joannem in carcere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέθηκεν καὶ τοῦτο ἐπὶ πᾶσιν καὶ κατέκλεισεν τὸν ἰωάννην ἐν τῇ φυλακῇ
verses.king_james: Added yet this above all, that he shut up John in prison.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem cum baptizaretur omnis populus, et Jesu baptizato, et orante, apertum est cælum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν τῷ βαπτισθῆναι ἅπαντα τὸν λαὸν καὶ ἰησοῦ βαπτισθέντος καὶ προσευχομένου ἀνεῳχθῆναι τὸν οὐρανὸν
verses.king_james: Now when all the people were baptized, it came to pass, that Jesus also being baptized, and praying, the heaven was opened,

verses.latin_vulgate: et descendit Spiritus Sanctus corporali specie sicut columba in ipsum: et vox de cælo facta est: Tu es filius meus dilectus, in te complacui mihi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καταβῆναι τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον σωματικῷ εἴδει ὡσεὶ περιστερὰν ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν καὶ φωνὴν ἐξ οὐρανοῦ γενέσθαι λέγουσαν, σὺ εἶ ὁ υἱός μου ὁ ἀγαπητός ἐν σοὶ ἠυδόκησα
verses.king_james: And the Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and a voice came from heaven, which said, Thou art my beloved Son; in thee I am well pleased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipse Jesus erat incipiens quasi annorum triginta, ut putabatur, filius Joseph, qui fuit Heli, qui fuit Mathat,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς ἦν ὁ ἰησοῦς ὡσεὶ ἐτῶν τριάκοντα ἀρχόμενος ὢν ὡς ἐνομίζετο υἱός ἰωσὴφ τοῦ ἠλὶ
verses.king_james: And Jesus himself began to be about thirty years of age, being (as was supposed) the son of Joseph, which was *the son* of Heli,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Levi, qui fuit Melchi, qui fuit Janne, qui fuit Joseph,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ματθὰτ, τοῦ λευὶ τοῦ μελχὶ τοῦ ἰαννὰ, τοῦ ἰωσὴφ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Matthat, which was *the son* of Levi, which was *the son* of Melchi, which was *the son* of Janna, which was *the son* of Joseph,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Mathathiæ, qui fuit Amos, qui fuit Nahum, qui fuit Hesli, qui fuit Nagge,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ματταθίου τοῦ ἀμὼς τοῦ ναοὺμ τοῦ ἑσλὶ τοῦ ναγγαὶ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Mattathias, which was *the son* of Amos, which was *the son* of Naum, which was *the son* of Esli, which was *the son* of Nagge,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Mahath, qui fuit Mathathiæ, qui fuit Semei, qui fuit Joseph, qui fuit Juda,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ μάαθ τοῦ ματταθίου τοῦ σεμεΐ, τοῦ ἰωσὴφ, τοῦ ἰουδὰ,
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Maath, which was *the son* of Mattathias, which was *the son* of Semei, which was *the son* of Joseph, which was *the son* of Juda,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Joanna, qui fuit Resa, qui fuit Zorobabel, qui fuit Salatheil, qui fuit Neri,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἰωὰννα τοῦ ῥησὰ τοῦ ξοροβαβὲλ τοῦ σαλαθιὴλ τοῦ νηρὶ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Joanna, which was *the son* of Rhesa, which was *the son* of Zorobabel, which was *the son* of Salathiel, which was *the son* of Neri,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Melchi, qui fuit Addi, qui fuit Cosan, qui fuit Elmadan, qui fuit Her,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ μελχὶ τοῦ ἀδδὶ τοῦ κωσὰμ τοῦ ἐλμωδὰμ, τοῦ ἢρ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Melchi, which was *the son* of Addi, which was *the son* of Cosam, which was *the son* of Elmodam, which was *the son* of Er,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Jesu, qui fuit Eliezer, qui fuit Jorim, qui fuit Mathat, qui fuit Levi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἰωσὴ, τοῦ ἐλιέζερ τοῦ ἰωρεὶμ, τοῦ ματθὰτ, τοῦ λευὶ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Jose, which was *the son* of Eliezer, which was *the son* of Jorim, which was *the son* of Matthat, which was *the son* of Levi,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Simeon, qui fuit Juda, qui fuit Joseph, qui fuit Jona, qui fuit Eliakim,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ συμεὼν τοῦ ἰούδα τοῦ ἰωσὴφ τοῦ ἰωνὰν, τοῦ ἐλιακεὶμ,
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Simeon, which was *the son* of Juda, which was *the son* of Joseph, which was *the son* of Jonan, which was *the son* of Eliakim,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Melea, qui fuit Menna, qui fuit Mathatha, qui fuit Natham, qui fuit David,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ μελεὰ τοῦ μαϊνάν τοῦ ματταθὰ τοῦ ναθὰν, τοῦ δαβὶδ,
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Melea, which was *the son* of Menan, which was *the son* of Mattatha, which was *the son* of Nathan, which was *the son* of David,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Jesse, qui fuit Obed, qui fuit Booz, qui fuit Salmon, qui fuit Naasson,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἰεσσαὶ τοῦ ὠβήδ, τοῦ βόοζ, τοῦ σαλμών, τοῦ ναασσὼν
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Jesse, which was *the son* of Obed, which was *the son* of Booz, which was *the son* of Salmon, which was *the son* of Naasson,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Aminadab, qui fuit Aram, qui fuit Esron, qui fuit Phares, qui fuit Judæ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἀμιναδὰβ τοῦ ἀράμ, τοῦ ἑσρὼμ τοῦ φάρες τοῦ ἰούδα
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Aminadab, which was *the son* of Aram, which was *the son* of Esrom, which was *the son* of Phares, which was *the son* of Juda,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Jacob, qui fuit Isaac, qui fuit Abrahæ, qui fuit Thare, qui fuit Nachor,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἰακὼβ τοῦ ἰσαὰκ τοῦ ἀβραὰμ τοῦ φάρα τοῦ ναχὼρ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Jacob, which was *the son* of Isaac, which was *the son* of Abraham, which was *the son* of Thara, which was *the son* of Nachor,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Sarug, qui fuit Ragau, qui fuit Phaleg, qui fuit Heber, qui fuit Sale,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ σαρούχ, τοῦ ῥαγαὺ τοῦ φάλεκ τοῦ ἕβερ τοῦ σαλὰ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Saruch, which was *the son* of Ragau, which was *the son* of Phalec, which was *the son* of Heber, which was *the son* of Sala,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Cainan, qui fuit Arphaxad, qui fuit Sem, qui fuit Noë, qui fuit Lamech,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ καϊνάν, τοῦ ἀρφαξὰδ τοῦ σὴμ τοῦ νῶε τοῦ λάμεχ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Cainan, which was *the son* of Arphaxad, which was *the son* of Sem, which was *the son* of Noe, which was *the son* of Lamech,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Methusale, qui fuit Henoch, qui fuit Jared, qui fuit Malaleel, qui fuit Cainan,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ μαθουσαλὰ τοῦ ἑνὼχ τοῦ ἰαρέδ, τοῦ μαλελεὴλ τοῦ καϊνὰν,
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Mathusala, which was *the son* of Enoch, which was *the son* of Jared, which was *the son* of Maleleel, which was *the son* of Cainan,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fuit Henos, qui fuit Seth, qui fuit Adam, qui fuit Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἐνὼς τοῦ σὴθ τοῦ ἀδὰμ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Which was *the son* of Enos, which was *the son* of Seth, which was *the son* of Adam, which was *the son* of God.
